demos.text: Having covered the “CR” in CRUD, we now turn to “U” for Update. Updating a resource closely resembles creating one, for both are multi-step affairs. First, the user requests a form for editing the data; next, the completed form is submitted. If no errors arise, the resource is duly updated. Otherwise, the form reappears with error messages, and the process begins anew until the submission succeeds.
demos.reasoning: The passage has a polished, highly structured instructional tone, explicitly decomposes the update workflow into sequential steps, and uses repetitive explanatory phrasing typical of AI-generated technical prose. This suggests it was generated by AI.

demos.text: You may, if you please, modify CMake variable settings before any build begins. Thus, for instance, this preliminary step allows the pre-detected directories for CuDNN or BLAS to be adjusted as need be.
demos.reasoning: The passage uses unusually formal and slightly awkward phrasing (“if you please,” “pre-detected directories,” and “as need be”) while presenting a generic explanatory statement. This combination is more characteristic of AI-generated paraphrasing than natural technical documentation.

signature.instructions: Decide whether the supplied passage was generated by an AI.

fields.description: True for AI-generated text; otherwise False
